perturbation.original_text: Payment by Bunker One for the Product, as defined in clause 2.1, will be made by wire transfer within three (3) Business Days after the date Vertex invoices Bunker One, to an account desig- nated from time to time by Vertex.
perturbation.changed_text: Payment by Bunker One for the Product, as defined in clause 2.1, will be made by wire transfer within fifteen (15) Business Days after the date Vertex invoices Bunker One, to an account desig- nated from time to time by Vertex.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by modifying the payment deadline. Section 4.1 originally stated payment should be made within three (3) business days, and now its fifteen (15) business days. This directly contradicts the enforcement of the initial payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment for any amounts due in accordance with the above shall be made within three (3) Business Days after the date of the applicable detailed ledger Statement. If any detailed ledger State- ment is disputed in good faith, the Party owing money will pay the undisputed portion by the due date and will by such due date provide a written explanation of the basis for the disputed portion to such due date. The Parties shall endeavor to resolve any disputes promptly, and the disputed amount found due, if any, plus accrued interest at the Interest Rate shall be paid within three (3) Business Days following resolution of the dispute. All payments under the terms of this Agreement shall be made in US Dollars and immediately available funds. The Parties shall net all undisputed amounts due and owing on the same day, and/or past due, arising under this Agreement such that the Party owing the greater amount shall make a single payment of the net amount to the other Party. Interest on late payments shall accrue at the Interest Rate from the due date until the date of payment.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated payment should be made within three (3) business days, and the changed text states it's fifteen (15) business days. The contradicted text explicitly requires payment within three (3) business days. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, rendering the payment terms ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a termination in accordance to clause 5.3 the Defaulting Party has to indemnify and hold harmless the Non-Defaulting Party for any and all losses, damages, costs etc. incurred by the Non-defaulting Party as a consequence of an Event of Default.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a termination in accordance to clause 5.3 the Defaulting Party will not indemnify and hold harmless the Non-Defaulting Party for any and all losses, damages, costs etc. incurred by the Non-defaulting Party as a consequence of an Event of Default, rather, this will be decided on a case-by-case basis.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct contradiction. Originally, clause 5.3 mandated that the Defaulting Party indemnify the Non-Defaulting Party. Now, the changed clause states that the Defaulting Party will not automatically indemnify the Non-Defaulting Party, and instead claims that it will be decided on a case-by-case basis. This undermines the initial agreement and creates uncertainty about the indemnification obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Bunker One shall promptly indemnify Vertex and pay any and all damages, losses, liabilities, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, incident to any suits, actions, investigations, claims or proceedings suffered, sustained, incurred or required to be paid by Vertex by reason of any negligence, gross negligence and/or intentional act or omission on the part of Bunker One in rendering services hereunder, or any breach or failure of observance or performance of any rep- resentation, warranty, covenant or agreement made by Bunker One hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that in the event of termination under clause 5.3, the Defaulting Party *must* indemnify the Non-Defaulting Party. The changed text removes this mandatory indemnification, stating instead that it will be decided on a case-by-case basis. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which mandates that Bunker One 'shall promptly indemnify Vertex and pay any and all damages, losses, liabilities, costs and expenses' under certain circumstances. The 'case-by-case' nature introduced by the changed text creates a clear inconsistency with the mandatory indemnification required in the Contradicted Text, thus introducing an in-text contradiction, making the terms of the contract unclear and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this JSMA, including without limitation any question regarding its existence, validity or termination, that is not resolved in accordance with the provisions set forth below in Section 13.3, shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama in the City of Mobile, unless that court does not have subject-matter jurisdiction or declines jurisdiction, in which case any such dispute shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the State Court for the State of Alabama, City of Mobile, as described in the following paragraphs. Any counterclaim arising out of, or in connection with, the dispute shall be brought in the same proceeding.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this JSMA, including without limitation any question regarding its existence, validity or termination, that is not resolved in accordance with the provisions set forth below in Section 13.3, shall be submitted to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the United States District Court for the Southern District of Alabama in the City of Mobile, unless that court does not have subject-matter jurisdiction or declines jurisdiction, in which case any such dispute shall be submitted to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the State Court for the State of Alabama, City of Mobile, as described in the following paragraphs. Any counterclaim arising out of, or in connection with, the dispute may be brought in any proceeding.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'exclusive jurisdiction' to 'non-exclusive jurisdiction,' and stating that any counterclaim 'may' be brought, instead of 'shall,' an in-text contradiction is created. The original clause mandates that disputes must be resolved in a specific court, whereas the modified term permits disputes to be resolved in another venue. This changes the nature of the dispute resolution process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) All deadlines specified in this Section 13.3 may be extended by mutual agreement. The procedures specified in this Section 13.3 are an essential provision of this Agreement and are legally binding on the parties. These procedures shall be the sole and exclusive procedures for the resolution of any Dispute between the parties arising out of or relating to this Agreement. Any and all actions to enforce the obligations under this Section 13.3 shall be brought in any court specified in Section 13.1.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'exclusive jurisdiction' and 'shall' regarding counterclaims, while the changed text introduces 'non-exclusive jurisdiction' and 'may' regarding counterclaims. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the procedures in Section 13.3 are 'the sole and exclusive procedures' and requires actions to enforce them be brought in a court specified in Section 13.1. The change allows disputes and counterclaims to be resolved elsewhere, negating the 'sole and exclusive' requirement and specified court. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as it fundamentally alters the dispute resolution process outlined in the contract and makes it unclear where a dispute may arise.
